title: corydalis curviflora subsp. minuticristata
intro: Corydalis curviflora subsp. minuticristata is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its small, curved flowers and delicate foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as dehydrocorybulbine and protopine. The medicinal properties of Corydalis curviflora subsp. minuticristata have been used to treat various health conditions, including pain, fever, and respiratory issues, offering relief from symptoms such as coughing and congestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tinctures, capsules, and teas, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
